mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over 60-day horizons across multiple recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index reflect immediate supply chain bottlenecks that compress profit margins, triggering rapid negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat following INDPRO declines exceeding 0.5% during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stationary federal funds rate indicate unanchored inflation expectations that force markets to price in future tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL accelerates while rates remain unchanged, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports wage-driven demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts below 1.4 million units signals weakening residential investment demand that reduces construction sector revenues and triggers negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of months where HOUST falls below the 1.4 million threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates an overheating economy where capacity utilization approaches limits, prompting negative S&P 500 forward returns as markets anticipate policy intervention.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward returns during late-cycle phases, invalidating the overheating premium hypothesis.
